info.title: Embroidery API
info.description:
Programmatic access to the embroidery pipeline. Convert an uploaded image into a machine-ready embroidery file (`out.zip` containing `.dst` / `.pes` / `.svg`).

Authenticate every request with your personal API key from the [embroidery testing playground](/embroidery).

tags.name: Generate
tags.description: Run the pipeline against an image you've previously uploaded via the playground.

tags.name: Catalog
tags.description: Read-only metadata: thread palettes, available manufacturers, allowed hoop sizes.

ApiKeyHeader.name: X-API-Key
ApiKeyHeader.description: Your personal API key (`pwsk_…`) issued from the embroidery page.

BearerAuth.description: Pass your personal API key as `Authorization: Bearer pwsk_…`.

Size.description: Allowed hoop size in inches. Determines the output canvas the pipeline targets.

number.description: Manufacturer's catalog number for the thread.

image.description: Image file. PNG or JPEG. Max 10 MB.

manufacturer.description: Thread catalog to quantize against. Defaults to `madeira-polyneon`. Use `GET /api/embroidery/palettes` to list valid slugs.

thread_numbers.description: Comma-separated catalog numbers (e.g. `1800,1812,1840`) restricting the pipeline to a subset of the manufacturer's threads. Omit to use the manufacturer's built-in default set — currently only defined for `madeira-polyneon` (the 45-color starter kit, product #924-45). For any other manufacturer, this field is required.

zipUrl.description: Public URL of the generated `out.zip` containing `.dst`, `.pes`, and `.svg` artifacts.

default.description: Manufacturer used when `manufacturer` is omitted on `/generate`.

manufacturers.description: All thread catalogs the pipeline can quantize against.

count.description: Number of threads in the catalog.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated but not allowed (e.g. URL not in your uploads, or service-key used where a per-user credential is required).

post.summary: Generate an embroidery zip from an uploaded image
post.description:
Upload an image and run the full pipeline (sample → palette select → trace → tag → convert) against it. Returns only the URL to the resulting `out.zip`.

**Defaults**:
- `manufacturer` → `madeira-polyneon`.
- `thread_numbers` → Madeira Polyneon 45-color starter kit (product #924-45). Other manufacturers have no built-in default — you must supply `thread_numbers` if you choose one.

**Constraints**:
- One concurrent generation per user on this endpoint.
- 3 generations per 30 days, accounted independently from the in-app playground.
- Repeat calls with the same image bytes + same size are deduplicated when no custom `manufacturer`/`thread_numbers` is provided. Custom-palette calls always re-run.
- Max 10 MB. PNG or JPEG.

200.description: Pipeline completed (or deduplicated to a prior result).

400.description: Bad request — missing or invalid field.

value.error: Unsupported image type image/gif; expected PNG or JPEG.

403.description: Credential resolves to no user (e.g. shared service key used here — needs a per-user key or session).

example.error: Per-user credential required (API key or session)

404.description: User record not found for the resolved credential.

409.description: You already have an in-flight generation on this endpoint.

example.error: You already have an API generation running. Wait for it to finish before submitting another.

429.description: Rate limited — quota exceeded or worker pool full. Honour `Retry-After`.

value.error: You've used 3 of 3 API generations in the past 30 days.
All worker slots busy. Retry after the Retry-After seconds.

502.description: Worker pipeline returned a transport error.

get.description: Returns the catalogs the pipeline can quantize against. Use the `default` value when you don't have a strong preference.

get.summary: Get the full thread list for a manufacturer
get.description: Returns every thread (hex, name, manufacturer number) in the named catalog. Useful if you want to constrain the pipeline to a specific subset of threads — narrow the catalog yourself, then pass the trimmed `thread_numbers` to the pipeline.
Returns every value accepted as `size` on `/api/embroidery/generate`. Sizes are inches, formatted as `WxH`.
